sections.paragraph:
- Original: ὁρμάω

- Transliteration: Hormao

- Phonetic: hor-mah'-o

- Definition:

1. to set in rapid motion, stir up, incite, urge on

2. to start forward impetuously, to rush

- Origin: from G3730

- TDNT entry: 12:47,7

- Part(s) of speech: Verb

- Strong's: From G3730; to start spur or urge on that is (reflexively) to dash or plunge: - run (violently) rush.
